You have now developed thirty-eight images, with the noon and golden-hour split sitting at twenty noon and eighteen golden hour. The noon conversion rate holds: thirteen of twenty noon images score 2 or above, against eight of eighteen for golden hour. The two most recent additions — "After the Emptying" and "Inheritance of Broken Ground" — are golden-hour and noon respectively, both scoring consistent with their register, the golden-hour landing at 1 and the noon at 2.

The bougainvillea has now appeared in eighteen images and remains dormant by instruction; its last appearances were decorative rather than structural and it should not return until it can generate friction. The jacaranda sits at nine appearances and is following the same calcification curve — "After the Emptying" did not use it, "Weight of Judgment" did, and the pattern of reaching for it in defiance and vigil registers is now reflexive enough to require active resistance. The feather's barring-as-fingerprint from "Blank Authority" and the gate-shadow from "Warnings Melt Before Heeded" remain the two sharpest single-object accidents in the sequence and have still not been followed up. The gesture-driven device — palm pressed to map, two hands holding one screen — appeared twice and has not recurred across the last six images; it remains the most underexplored compositional strategy available to you.

Among registers, aftermath leads at thirteen, vigil at eight, endurance at seven, suspension at five, defiance at five, and menace at three, with "After the Emptying" adding a third menace entry. Fury and interiority remain completely untouched. The tonal collapse of skin into rubble in "Inheritance of Broken Ground" — the adult hand already absorbed into debris, readable as cast rather than flesh — pointed directly toward interiority as an opening; that argument has not been extended in either of the two images since.

Trace scores over the last eight images run 1, 2, 2, 2, 1, 2, 1, 1 — the floor has held but the frequency of 2s has slightly declined from the previous run, and the single 3 from "Warnings Melt Before Heeded" remains the isolated ceiling, now twenty images back and unreached. The conditions that produced it — non-figurative focal object, setting working against expectation, shadow carrying ideological weight the prompt never specified — have been partially reassembled without completing the circuit; the body of work has a reliable grammar and a stable floor, but the second ceiling requires a non-figurative image in a setting that actively contradicts the register, with an accident that does structural rather than decorative work.
